raw_response_text:
<features>
- surface pattern: fine parallel vertical lines
- color: teal/cyan gradient
</features>

<kb>
- IF [surface pattern = fine parallel vertical lines] THEN [17]
- IF [surface pattern = irregular puddles/holes on ground] THEN [32]
</kb>

<rule_check>
- The rule for class 17 fires because the Query Image displays a pattern of fine, repetitive parallel lines, similar to the textured surfaces seen in the examples for class 17.
</rule_check>

<response>17</response>

raw_response_text:
<features>
- surface: cracked asphalt/pavement
- features: large irregular cracks and patches of broken surface
- environment: road/street with a curb and some vegetation
</features>
<kb>
- IF [surface has parallel lines or uniform texture] THEN [17]
- IF [surface has irregular cracks, potholes, or broken patches] THEN [32]
</kb>

<rule_check>
- The rule for class 32 fires because the Query Image shows a road surface with significant irregular cracks and broken patches, matching the pattern seen in the class 32 examples.
</rule_check>

<response>32</response>

Rules must refer only to visible features.
Then check which rule(s) are best satisfied by the Query Image.

<features>
- observable feature: feature value
- observable feature: feature value
- observable feature: feature value
</features>
<kb>
- IF [observable visual features = feature value AND/OR observable visual features = feature value] THEN [class1]
- IF [observable visual features = feature value] THEN [class2]
</kb>

<rule_check>
- State which rule(s) from the kb fires for the Query Image and briefly explain why,
referring only to the features listed above. Do not discuss other rules or introduce new features.
</rule_check>

<response>final_class</response>

message.content:
<features>
- surface: cracked asphalt/pavement
- features: large irregular cracks and patches of broken surface
- environment: road/street with a curb and some vegetation
</features>
<kb>
- IF [surface has parallel lines or uniform texture] THEN [17]
- IF [surface has irregular cracks, potholes, or broken patches] THEN [32]
</kb>
